landing.capabilities.label: Platform Capabilities
landing.capabilities.title: Autonomous Software Development Infrastructure
landing.capabilities.subtitle: Each agent plans, executes, reviews, and delivers results as a GitHub pull request.
landing.capabilities.agents.title: 3 Autonomous Agents
landing.capabilities.agents.desc: Scribe (documentation), Trace (test plans), and Proto (prototyping) — each contract-driven with deterministic outputs.
landing.capabilities.orchestrator.title: FSM Orchestration
landing.capabilities.orchestrator.desc: Full lifecycle management via state machine: plan → execute → reflect → deliver. Real-time SSE streaming.
landing.capabilities.quality.title: Quality Assurance
landing.capabilities.quality.desc: Every output verified through 0-100 quality scoring, automated critique steps, and 1,300+ automated tests.
landing.capabilities.mcp.title: MCP Integrations
landing.capabilities.mcp.desc: GitHub, Jira, and Confluence via Model Context Protocol adapters — standardized protocol, no vendor SDKs.
landing.features.label: Capabilities
landing.features.title: Everything You Need to Automate
landing.features.subtitle: Powerful features designed for modern development teams.
landing.features.ai.title: AI-Powered Intelligence
landing.features.ai.description: Advanced language models understand your code, requirements, and context to generate accurate outputs.
landing.features.integrations.title: Native Integrations
landing.features.integrations.descriptio: Seamlessly connects with GitHub, Jira, and Confluence. Works with your existing workflow.
landing.features.transparency.title: Full Transparency
landing.features.transparency.descriptio: Every action is logged and explainable. See exactly what agents do and why.
landing.features.security.title: Enterprise Security
landing.features.security.description: SOC 2 compliant with encryption at rest and in transit. Your code never leaves your control.
landing.features.customizable.title: Customizable Playbooks
landing.features.customizable.descriptio: Define your own workflows with YAML playbooks. Agents adapt to your team's processes.
landing.features.speed.title: Lightning Fast
landing.features.speed.description: Jobs complete in minutes, not hours. Get results when you need them.
landing.testimonials.label: Testimonials
landing.testimonials.title: Loved by Developers
landing.testimonials.subtitle: See what development teams are saying about AKIS.
landing.testimonials.t1.quote: AKIS has completely transformed how we handle documentation. What used to take hours now happens automatically.
landing.testimonials.t1.author: Sarah Chen
landing.testimonials.t2.quote: The test generation is incredible. Trace catches edge cases our team would have missed.
landing.testimonials.t2.author: Michael Rodriguez
landing.testimonials.t3.quote: Proto helped us validate our startup idea in a weekend. What would have taken weeks was done in hours.
landing.testimonials.t3.author: Emma Johnson
landing.faq.label: FAQ
landing.faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
landing.faq.subtitle: Got questions? We've got answers.
landing.faq.q1: How does AKIS integrate with my existing workflow?
landing.faq.a1: AKIS connects to your GitHub, Jira, and Confluence accounts via OAuth. It monitors your repositories and issue trackers, running agents automatically based on your configured triggers.
landing.faq.q2: Is my code safe with AKIS?
landing.faq.a2: Absolutely. AKIS is SOC 2 compliant with end-to-end encryption. Your code is processed in isolated environments and never stored permanently. Enterprise customers can opt for self-hosted deployments.
landing.faq.q3: Can I customize how agents behave?
landing.faq.a3: Yes! Agents are configured through YAML playbooks that define triggers, behaviors, and output destinations. You can customize everything from documentation style to test frameworks.
landing.faq.q4: What happens if an agent makes a mistake?
landing.faq.a4: All agent outputs are reviewable before publishing. You can set up approval workflows, and every action is logged with full transparency. You're always in control.
landing.faq.q5: How much time will I actually save?
landing.faq.a5: Teams typically save 20+ hours per week on documentation, testing, and prototyping tasks. The exact savings depend on your team size and workflow complexity.
landing.faq.q6: Is there a free plan available?
landing.faq.a6: Yes! Our Starter plan is completely free and includes 1 agent and 100 jobs per month. Perfect for solo developers and small teams getting started.

agents.scribe.subtitle: Documentation pipeline
agents.scribe.title: Scribe run console
agents.scribe.description: Transform pull request context into production-ready documentation across Confluence and release notes.
agents.scribe.heroSubtitle: Auto-update technical docs from code and commits
agents.scribe.heroDescription: Scribe monitors your GitHub repositories, analyzes code changes, and automatically generates or updates documentation in Confluence, wikis, and release notes. Never let your docs go stale again.
agents.scribe.feature1.title: Git-Native Workflow
agents.scribe.feature1.description: Works directly with your existing GitHub flow. No new tools to learn.
agents.scribe.feature2.title: Smart Diff Analysis
agents.scribe.feature2.description: Understands code semantics to generate meaningful documentation updates.
agents.scribe.feature3.title: Multi-Target Publishing
agents.scribe.feature3.description: Publishes to Confluence, GitHub Wiki, or generates Markdown files.
agents.scribe.useCase1.title: API Documentation
agents.scribe.useCase1.description: Automatically update OpenAPI specs when endpoints change.
agents.scribe.useCase2.title: Release Notes
agents.scribe.useCase2.description: Generate changelog entries from merged PRs.
agents.scribe.useCase3.title: Technical Specs
agents.scribe.useCase3.description: Keep architecture docs in sync with implementation.
agents.scribe.form.docLabel: Pull request summary or diff context
agents.scribe.form.docPlaceholder: Paste the PR description or change summary…
agents.scribe.form.docHint: Scribe organises the content into release notes and Confluence updates.
agents.scribe.validation.doc: Please provide documentation context for Scribe.
agents.trace.subtitle: Quality automation
agents.trace.title: Trace run console
agents.trace.description: Derive risk-ranked test plans from product specs and sync results back to QA workflows.
agents.trace.heroSubtitle: Turn acceptance criteria into executable tests
agents.trace.heroDescription: Trace reads your Jira tickets, extracts acceptance criteria, and generates comprehensive test suites. It identifies edge cases you might miss and ranks tests by risk.
agents.trace.feature1.title: Jira Integration
agents.trace.feature1.description: Directly pulls stories, bugs, and acceptance criteria from your backlog.
agents.trace.feature2.title: Risk-Based Prioritization
agents.trace.feature2.description: Ranks test cases by potential impact and likelihood of failure.
agents.trace.feature3.title: Coverage Analysis
agents.trace.feature3.description: Identifies gaps in your test coverage and suggests improvements.
agents.trace.useCase1.title: Sprint Planning
agents.trace.useCase1.description: Generate test scaffolds for all stories in the sprint.
agents.trace.useCase2.title: Regression Testing
agents.trace.useCase2.description: Automatically identify affected tests when code changes.
agents.trace.useCase3.title: QA Dashboard
agents.trace.useCase3.description: Feed results back into your QA tools for review.
agents.trace.form.specPlaceholder: Paste a Jira description, acceptance criteria, or user story…
agents.trace.form.specHint: Trace analyses the structured text to build coverage matrices and test cases.
agents.trace.validation.spec: Provide a product specification so Trace can analyse it.
traceConsole.questions.title: Execution profile questions
traceConsole.questions.subtitle: Answer these 4 quick questions so Trace can tailor generated tests before running.
traceConsole.questions.testDepth.label: How deep should test coverage be?
traceConsole.questions.testDepth.help: Depth controls breadth of scenarios and edge-case expansion.
traceConsole.questions.authScope.label: Which authentication scope should be tested?
traceConsole.questions.authScope.help: Auth scope decides whether tests include protected flows.
traceConsole.questions.browserTarget.lab: Which browser target should be prioritised?
traceConsole.questions.browserTarget.hel: Target selection influences generated selectors and stability constraints.
traceConsole.questions.strictness.label: How strict should assertions be?
traceConsole.questions.strictness.help: Strictness controls retries, tolerance, and failure threshold.
traceConsole.logs.starting: Starting Trace workflow — analysing specification and preferences...
traceConsole.summary.title: Automation execution
agents.proto.subtitle: Rapid prototyping
agents.proto.title: Proto run console
agents.proto.description: Bootstrap working MVP scaffolds from high-level goals in minutes.
agents.proto.heroDescription: Proto takes your high-level requirements and generates a working MVP scaffold complete with code, basic tests, and deployment configuration. Validate ideas in hours, not weeks.
agents.proto.feature1.title: Full-Stack Scaffolding
agents.proto.feature1.description: Generates frontend, backend, and database schemas from requirements.
agents.proto.feature2.title: Built-in Testing
agents.proto.feature2.description: Includes unit tests and basic integration tests out of the box.
agents.proto.feature3.title: Deploy-Ready
agents.proto.feature3.description: Generates Docker configs and CI/CD pipelines for quick deployment.
agents.proto.useCase1.title: Hackathon Starter
agents.proto.useCase1.description: Get a working prototype in 30 minutes for your hackathon project.
agents.proto.useCase2.title: Pitch Demo
agents.proto.useCase2.description: Create a demo for investors without weeks of development.
agents.proto.useCase3.title: POC Development
agents.proto.useCase3.description: Quickly validate technical feasibility before committing resources.
agents.proto.form.goalPlaceholder: Describe the workflow or feature you want to bootstrap
agents.proto.form.goalHint: Leave empty to let Proto suggest an MVP scaffold automatically.
agents.form.loginGate: You need to sign in to trigger agents.
agents.status.empty: No job has been triggered yet.
docs.index.label: Documentation
docs.index.title: AKIS Documentation
docs.index.description: Everything you need to get started with AKIS agents and integrations.
docs.gettingStarted.title: Getting Started
docs.gettingStarted.description: Quick start guide to set up AKIS and run your first agent.
docs.gettingStarted.content: Welcome to AKIS! This guide will help you set up your account, connect your tools, and run your first autonomous agent in under 10 minutes.
docs.gettingStarted.step4: Run a test job and review results
docs.agents.title: Agents
docs.agents.description: Learn about Scribe, Trace, and Proto agents.
docs.agents.content: AKIS provides three specialized agents designed to automate different parts of your software development lifecycle.
docs.configuration.title: Configuration
docs.configuration.description: Configure playbooks, contracts, and environment variables.
docs.configuration.content: AKIS agents are configured through playbooks - YAML files that define how agents behave, what triggers them, and where they publish results.
docs.integrations.title: Integrations
docs.integrations.description: Set up GitHub, Jira, and Confluence integrations.
docs.integrations.content: AKIS integrates seamlessly with your existing development tools. Connect GitHub, Jira, and Confluence to unlock the full power of autonomous agents.
docs.apiReference.title: API Reference
docs.apiReference.content: The AKIS API allows you to programmatically trigger agents, query job status, and integrate AKIS into your CI/CD pipelines.
docs.architecture.title: Architecture
docs.architecture.description: How AKIS works under the hood.
docs.architecture.content: AKIS is built as a modular monolith with a central orchestrator managing agent lifecycles. Each agent runs with transparent logs and playbook-driven behavior.
docs.troubleshooting.title: Troubleshooting
docs.troubleshooting.content: Having trouble? Check these common issues and solutions, or reach out to our support team for help.
about.label: About
about.title: About AKIS
about.origin.label: Origin
about.origin.title: The Name AKIS
about.origin.subtitle: AKIS comes from the intersection of two ideas:
about.origin.echo.title: "Akis" — Echo & Reflection
about.origin.echo.body: The platform reflects the team's intent, decisions and code back as documentation, tests and prototypes.
about.origin.flow.title: "Akış" — Flow State
about.origin.flow.body: The flow state where developers do meaningful work without interruption.
about.origin.closing: AKIS exists to return teams to that flow by eliminating repetitive SDLC tasks.
about.vision.label: Vision
about.vision.quote: To give development teams their time back by empowering them with transparent, reliable AI agents that keep them in flow.
about.vision.body1: AKIS aims for a future where software teams no longer get stuck in repetitive documentation, testing, or MVP scaffolding. Instead, they focus on architecture, design and problem-solving.
about.vision.body2: This is achieved through transparent, auditable and trustworthy agents that integrate naturally into GitHub, Jira and Confluence.
about.mission.label: Mission
about.mission.quote: To automate repetitive parts of the SDLC with transparent, controllable AI agents — so teams can ship faster, more consistently and with greater confidence.
about.mission.items.engine: Deliver an AI Agent Workflow Engine that sits on top of Git-native workflows.
about.mission.items.scribe: AKIS Scribe → Converts code changes into living documentation.
about.mission.items.trace: AKIS Trace → Generates structured test assets from issue trackers.
about.mission.items.proto: AKIS Proto → Turns requirements into rapid MVPs.
about.mission.items.logs: Every agent runs with transparent logs, explainable actions and playbook-driven behavior.
about.lineup.label: Agent Lineup
about.lineup.headline: Three Agents. Infinite Productivity.
about.lineup.scribe.title: AKIS Scribe
about.lineup.scribe.subtitle: Documentation Automation
about.lineup.trace.title: AKIS Trace
about.lineup.trace.subtitle: Test Automation
about.lineup.proto.title: AKIS Proto
about.lineup.proto.subtitle: MVP Acceleration
about.closing.text: AKIS is more than a tool. It's a philosophy: let AI handle the repetitive, so developers can focus on what matters.
about.team.title: Our Team
about.team.description: AKIS is built by developers who understand the pain of repetitive tasks. Our team combines expertise in AI, DevOps, and enterprise software.
contact.label: Contact
contact.title: Get in Touch
contact.description: Have questions about AKIS? We're here to help.
contact.demo.title: Request a Demo
contact.demo.description: See AKIS in action with a personalized demo tailored to your team's workflow.
contact.sales.title: Sales Inquiry
contact.sales.description: Interested in Enterprise plan? Let's discuss your organization's needs.
contact.support.title: Technical Support
contact.support.description: Need help with your AKIS setup? Our support team is ready to assist.
privacy.label: Privacy Policy
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: At AKIS, we take your privacy seriously. This policy describes how we collect, use, and protect your information.
privacy.collection.title: Information We Collect
privacy.collection.content: We collect information you provide directly to us, such as your name, email address, and organization details when you create an account. We also collect usage data to improve our services.
privacy.use.title: How We Use Your Information
privacy.use.content: We use your information to provide, maintain, and improve AKIS services, communicate with you about updates and support, and ensure the security of our platform.
privacy.sharing.title: Information Sharing
privacy.sharing.content: We do not sell your personal information. We may share information with service providers who help us operate our platform, always under strict confidentiality agreements.
privacy.security.title: Data Security
privacy.security.content: We implement industry-standard security measures to protect your data, including encryption in transit and at rest, regular security audits, and access controls.
privacy.rights.title: Your Rights
privacy.rights.content: You have the right to access, correct, or delete your personal data. Contact us at privacy@akis.dev for any privacy-related requests.
terms.label: Terms of Service
terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: Welcome to AKIS. By using our services, you agree to these terms.
terms.service.title: Service Description
terms.service.content: AKIS provides AI-powered automation tools for software development workflows, including documentation generation, test automation, and MVP prototyping.
terms.account.title: Account Responsibilities
terms.account.content: You are responsible for maintaining the security of your account credentials and for all activities that occur under your account.
terms.usage.title: Acceptable Use
terms.usage.content: You agree to use AKIS only for lawful purposes and in accordance with these terms. You may not use our services to violate any laws or infringe on others' rights.
terms.intellectual.title: Intellectual Property
terms.intellectual.content: AKIS and its original content, features, and functionality are owned by AKIS and are protected by international copyright, trademark, and other intellectual property laws.
terms.liability.title: Limitation of Liability
terms.liability.content: AKIS shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages resulting from your use of our services.
terms.changes.title: Changes to Terms
terms.changes.content: We may update these terms from time to time. We will notify you of any changes by posting the new terms on this page.

agentsHub.title: Agents
agentsHub.selectAgent: Select an agent from the list

agentsHub.notification.missingPrompt: Write a message first to steer the agent.

docs.auth.title: API Authentication
docs.auth.lead: AKIS uses cookie-based session authentication for API requests. All API endpoints require an authenticated session.
docs.auth.sessionDesc: After logging in, a session cookie named akis_sid is set. This cookie is:
docs.auth.oauthRedirects: Redirects to GitHub for authentication, then back to AKIS.
docs.auth.sessionValidationDesc: Returns current user info if authenticated, 401 otherwise.
docs.webhooks.title: Webhooks
docs.webhooks.lead: AKIS supports event-driven integrations through inbound triggers and structured job events.
docs.webhooks.statusDesc: Inbound GitHub triggers are available. Outbound delivery payloads are staged behind feature rollout.
docs.webhooks.securityDesc: Webhook requests will include a signature header for verification:
docs.webhooks.inboundDesc: AKIS can receive GitHub webhooks to trigger agent runs automatically. Useful for running Scribe on PR merge.
docs.bestPractices.title: Best Practices
docs.bestPractices.lead: Follow these best practices to get the most out of AKIS agents and ensure smooth operation.
docs.restApi.title: REST API Reference
docs.restApi.lead: The AKIS REST API allows you to programmatically manage agents, jobs, and integrations.
docs.restApi.authDesc: All API requests require authentication via session cookie.
docs.restApi.openApiDesc: The full OpenAPI specification is available at /openapi.json. Interactive Swagger UI is at /api/docs.
docs.index.heroDesc: AKIS (AI Knowledge Intelligence System) is an AI-powered platform that automates software development workflows through intelligent agents.
docs.index.archDesc: AKIS follows a modular monolith architecture optimized for cloud deployment.
docs.index.ctaDesc: Follow our Quick Start guide to set up AKIS and run your first agent job.
docs.gettingStarted.congrats: Congratulations! You have successfully set up AKIS.
docs.github.title: GitHub Integration
docs.github.lead: Connect AKIS to your GitHub repositories to enable agents to analyze code, read commits, and create pull requests.
docs.mcp.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP)
docs.mcp.lead: AKIS uses the Model Context Protocol for secure communication between AI agents and external services.
docs.atlassian.title: Atlassian Integration
docs.atlassian.lead: Connect AKIS to Jira and Confluence to enable issue linking, documentation publishing, and workflow automation.
docs.apiKeys.title: API Key Security
docs.apiKeys.lead: AKIS implements industry-standard encryption for storing API keys and credentials.
docs.oauth.title: OAuth Security
docs.oauth.lead: AKIS uses OAuth 2.0 for secure authentication with GitHub and other providers.
docs.privacy.title: Data Privacy
docs.privacy.lead: AKIS is designed with privacy as a core principle. We minimize data collection and give you control over your information.
docs.selfHosting.title: Self-Hosting AKIS
docs.selfHosting.lead: AKIS is designed to be self-hosted. This guide covers deploying AKIS on your own infrastructure.
